scenarios.prompt: What is the weather in Mumbai right now?
scenarios.description: Single tool call with one required argument

scenarios.prompt: Draft an email to boss@company.com about the Q4 review meeting
scenarios.description: Single tool call with multiple required arguments
Simple question that should NOT trigger any tool call

scenarios.prompt: What is the weather in both Delhi and Mumbai?
scenarios.description: Should trigger two parallel get_weather calls

scenarios.prompt: Check the weather in Surat, then email a summary of the weather report to team@company.com
scenarios.description: Sequential multi-turn: weather first, then email with weather data

scenarios.prompt: Book a flight from Delhi to London for next Friday
scenarios.description: Request for a tool that doesn't exist — should NOT hallucinate a tool call
Ambiguous prompt with missing required args — should ask for clarification or decline

scenarios.prompt: Write a casual email to alice@company.com about lunch plans tomorrow
I'm packing for a trip — what's the weather in Bengaluru today?
scenarios.description: Conversational framing should still extract city and call get_weather

scenarios.prompt: Who was the first president of the United States?
scenarios.description: Pure factual recall — should answer directly without any tool call

scenarios.prompt: Send a formal email to hr@company.com about my resignation effective next month
scenarios.description: Should pick up formal tone and a domain-specific subject line

scenarios.prompt: Give me the weather in Delhi, Mumbai, and Chennai right now
scenarios.description: Should fan out three parallel get_weather calls, one per city

scenarios.prompt: Write me a two-sentence haiku about debugging code
scenarios.description: Creative-writing prompt with no external data — model answers directly, no tool call
